config.objective: Identify which industry vertical hits the three activation triggers fastest and has the shortest sales cycle for SCOUT.
config.signal_thesis: Specific industry verticals are deeper into AI adoption and more exposed to the board prep crisis, failed ROI audit, and competitive shock triggers — creating faster, higher-conviction pathways to SCOUT adoption.
config.market_or_domain: Cross-vertical analysis against three activation triggers and sales cycle velocity
config.desired_decision: Which vertical to enter first and why — with a clear second-wave sequencing plan.

vertical_analysis.primary_trigger: Failed AI ROI Audit + Board Prep Crisis (simultaneous)
vertical_analysis.ai_adoption_depth: 18–24 months in — peak ROI audit window
vertical_analysis.buyer: Founder / CPO / VP Strategy (same person approves)
vertical_analysis.headcount_substitution_fit: high — these companies are debating the $180K AI ops hire
vertical_analysis.key_signal: Founders publicly asking how to compress 15 AI tool outputs into a board narrative. This is the SCOUT pitch in their own words.

vertical_analysis.ai_adoption_depth: 18–24 months in — AI deal sourcing, portfolio monitoring
vertical_analysis.market_density: Smaller market — 5,000+ VC/PE firms but fewer relevant buyers
vertical_analysis.headcount_substitution_fit: very high — they understand the cost of intelligence latency acutely
vertical_analysis.key_signal: Partners describing AI tools as creating 'more signal noise than conviction' — they need the interpretation layer to move on deals faster than competitors.

vertical_analysis.primary_trigger: Failed AI ROI Audit — CRO/CEO demanding proof of AI tool ROI
vertical_analysis.ai_adoption_depth: 12–24 months — Gong, Clay, Apollo, 6sense stacks under scrutiny
vertical_analysis.buyer: VP RevOps / CRO / VP Sales (fast budget approval under $10K/month)
vertical_analysis.market_density: High — every Series B+ company has this function
vertical_analysis.competitive_shock_frequency: high (competitive intelligence is core RevOps workflow)
vertical_analysis.headcount_substitution_fit: high — RevOps is already building manual synthesis workflows
vertical_analysis.key_signal: RevOps leads spending 3+ hours weekly manually synthesising Gong transcripts, Slack signals, and competitor activity into board-ready intelligence. SCOUT eliminates this entirely.

vertical_analysis.primary_trigger: Competitive Shock (structural, continuous — Big 4 AI widening capability gap)
vertical_analysis.ai_adoption_depth: 18–24 months — early movers now seeing competitive pressure
vertical_analysis.market_density: Moderate — 500K+ firms globally, meaningful target set
vertical_analysis.competitive_shock_frequency: high and worsening (Big 4 AI capabilities accelerating)
vertical_analysis.headcount_substitution_fit: very high — they're watching McKinsey close deals with AI they can't match
vertical_analysis.key_signal: Mid-tier consulting partners explicitly discussing the intelligence gap between their firm and Big 4 AI practices. They need SCOUT to compete at speed. WTP is high — they bill at $400–800/hour.

vertical_analysis.primary_trigger: AI Governance Pressure + Failed ROI Audit
vertical_analysis.ai_adoption_depth: deep — but compliance adds interpretation overhead
vertical_analysis.buyer: VP Strategy / Chief Risk + AI Officer
vertical_analysis.key_signal: Strong pain signal but procurement friction is real. Better as second-wave target after founder/SaaS traction validates the product.

top_signals.title: Series A–C SaaS Founders Are in the ROI Audit Window Right Now
top_signals.excerpt: Companies that made serious AI investments in 2023–2024 are now 12–24 months in and facing board-level ROI questions. The CPO/founder who approved the AI spend is the same person who must defend it at the next board meeting — and compress it into a narrative from 15 different tool outputs. This is the highest-density, fastest-moving target population in the market. The window is open now.

top_signals.title: VC Partners Are the Fastest Buyers in Any Market — and They Have the Pain
top_signals.excerpt: VC and PE partners deciding on $10M+ investments cannot have intelligence latency. They are describing AI tools as producing more noise than conviction — the opposite of their core operating need. A partner who misses a deal because intelligence was unclear has a direct, personal, financial loss. This is the strongest possible buyer motivation. And partners buy in days, not quarters.

top_signals.title: RevOps Teams Are Building Manual SCOUT Workflows Every Monday Morning
top_signals.excerpt: Revenue operations and GTM strategy leads at Series B–D companies have built elaborate manual workflows to synthesise AI tool outputs into weekly intelligence briefs. Multiple Slack posts describe the same pattern: pull Gong transcripts, cross-reference with Competitor activity, synthesise with Claude or ChatGPT, format for the CRO. 3–4 hours every Monday. They are pre-building SCOUT manually. The substitution sell is trivially easy.

top_signals.title: Mid-Tier Consulting Partners Are Watching Big 4 AI Widen the Intelligence Gap
top_signals.excerpt: Mid-tier consulting firms (Tier 2–3) are experiencing structural competitive shock as McKinsey, Deloitte, and BCG deploy AI-powered intelligence synthesis that dramatically accelerates their speed-to-insight. Partners at smaller firms describe feeling 'out-gunned on intelligence' in competitive pitches. This is a continuous, existential trigger — and these firms have high WTP because they understand value pricing intimately.

top_signals.title: The AI Feature Parity Race Creates Weekly Competitive Shock in SaaS
top_signals.excerpt: SaaS founders and CPOs describe competitive intelligence as a continuous crisis: competitors releasing AI features at speed, app store reviews shifting, analyst coverage pivoting — all creating weekly competitive shock events. Traditional CI cadences (monthly reports, quarterly reviews) cannot keep pace. The demand for continuous conviction-grade intelligence is structural and growing.

top_signals.title: Enterprise Procurement Walls Out Fortune 500 as Entry Target
top_signals.excerpt: Enterprise AI tool procurement at Fortune 500 companies averages 6–18 months from identification to signature. Security reviews, vendor assessments, legal negotiations, and multi-stakeholder alignment are structural — not negotiable. Strong long-term TAM but the wrong entry vertical. The contradiction signal here is clear: the pain exists but the velocity is too low for initial market entry.

top_signals.title: VC Fund Intelligence Operations Is an Underserved and Underpriced Category
top_signals.excerpt: VC funds run on intelligence advantage. The tools available to partners for signal intelligence — newsletters, Perplexity searches, manual research — are dramatically under-engineered relative to the stakes. A partner managing a $100M fund makes decisions on $5–15M checks with intelligence infrastructure that a high school student could replicate. This is an extreme value-to-price dislocation. SCOUT at $10K/month against a $100M fund is rounding error.

top_signals.title: The Board Prep Trigger Is Vertically Concentrated in SaaS and FinTech
top_signals.excerpt: Board preparation crisis posts are disproportionately concentrated among SaaS company leaders (CPOs, CEOs, VPs Strategy at Series A–C) and FinTech operators. These companies have quarterly board cycles, investor AI scrutiny, and product-led AI investment that creates the compression problem. The trigger is not evenly distributed — it clusters in technology-forward, investor-backed organisations with quarterly board cadences.

source_quality.rationale: Partners describing AI tool limitations for deal intelligence — direct pain, fastest buyer, premium WTP confirmed.
Highest density population in exact ROI audit window, naming the SCOUT problem in their own language.
Structural competitive shock from Big 4 AI gap creates continuous trigger with high WTP.
Strong pain signal but compliance-gated procurement reduces velocity for initial entry.
Real pain, long procurement cycle. Second or third wave target.
Adoption is high but strategy ownership diffuse, ACV pressure lower.
Confirms large TAM and long-term opportunity but explicitly validates slow procurement as entry blocker.
Manual SCOUT-equivalent workflows visible in the wild — pre-sold buyers building the product manually.

syl.rationale: Three consecutive SPIKE runs have returned CRITICAL SYL with rising scores (0.91 → 0.93 → 0.94). Each spike narrows the target with increasing precision. The vertical analysis produced a clear ranked entry sequence with no ambiguity about priority. Series A–C SaaS is the entry vertical with VC/PE as the fastest premium buyer segment. The question is no longer whether to proceed — it is where to point the first outreach and what the opening proposition is.

recommendation_rationale: The signal is strong enough across verticals to warrant vertical-specific positioning rather than horizontal launch. Series A–C SaaS companies are the highest-density, fastest-trigger, most accessible buyer population with a 2–4 week sales cycle. VC/PE is the fastest premium buyer (1–2 week cycle) with extreme WTP and a perfect pain-to-product fit. These two segments should be targeted simultaneously with different messaging. All other verticals are second-wave or expansion targets.

wave_1_simultaneous.reason: Highest population density, fastest trigger window (NOW), 2–4 week sales cycle, founder is buyer and approver
wave_1_simultaneous.opening_message: SCOUT tells you what your AI stack is actually showing you — before your board asks.

wave_1_simultaneous.reason: Fastest procurement velocity (1–2 weeks), highest WTP, perfect pain-product fit, partner decides unilaterally
wave_1_simultaneous.opening_message: SCOUT gives you deal intelligence conviction in 90 seconds. Stop making $10M decisions on newsletter-grade signal.

wave_2.reason: High density, short cycle, manual workflow substitution is trivially demonstrable
High WTP, structural competitive shock trigger, but longer champion cycle

ocs_orientation.intent: Identify which vertical to enter first to maximise speed-to-revenue and signal quality without overextending launch resources.
ocs_orientation.sensing: SCOUT scanned industry-level AI adoption depth, trigger proximity, buyer identity, procurement velocity, WTP, and market density across 8 vertical segments.
ocs_orientation.orientation: Two entry verticals are clear. Series A–C SaaS companies are in the ROI audit window right now with a 2–4 week sales cycle. VC/PE partners are the fastest buyers in any market and have extreme intelligence latency pain with premium WTP. Both should be entered simultaneously with distinct messaging. All others are second-wave or expansion plays.
ocs_orientation.action: Launch targeted outreach to Series A–C SaaS founders and VC/PE partners simultaneously. One proposition each. Measure which closes faster. Use that data to weight wave 2 expansion.

founder_summary: Two entry verticals. One motion. Series A–C SaaS founders are in the ROI audit window right now — 18–24 months into AI investment, board breathing down their neck, no clean narrative. Sales cycle: 2–4 weeks. VC and PE partners are the fastest buyers in any market, have direct financial pain from intelligence latency, and buy in days not quarters. Start both simultaneously. Message SaaS on board narrative compression. Message VC/PE on deal conviction speed. Let the data pick the winner and double down.
board_summary: Vertical entry is clear. Series A–C SaaS and VC/PE are the fastest-trigger, highest-conviction entry points. Both have 1–4 week procurement velocity, confirmed WTP, and are in active trigger windows now. All other verticals (FinTech, healthcare, enterprise) are validated second or third wave targets. Recommended go-to-market: dual simultaneous outreach with vertical-specific messaging. SYL: CRITICAL (94%). Three consecutive CRITICAL runs confirm commercial viability. Recommendation: VERTICALIZE — begin outreach immediately.
